summary:
Arm blood pressure differences greater than 15 mmHg suggest suspect presence of subclanian steal syndrome but are insufficient for predicting disease severity due to poor diagnostic accuracy. The definitive assessment of severity relies on Doppler measures such as peak systolic velocity rather than simple BP measurements.  

\boxed{\text{B}}
